name: Malaekahana Beach
tagline: A mile of empty gold sand with a tiny island you can walk to at low tide — one of Oahu's best-kept secrets.
parking: Free state park lot off Kamehameha Hwy
hours: Open daily, 7:00 AM – 7:45 PM (gates locked at closing)
bestTime: Low tide to walk to Goat Island (Mokuauia); check tide charts before going
talkStory: Malaekahana means 'song-entwined' in Hawaiian, and this mile-long stretch of golden sand is the quietest beach on Oahu's north-facing coast. At low tide you can wade to Mokuauia (Goat Island), a state bird sanctuary with its own tiny hidden beach on the far side — wear reef shoes for the rocky crossing. Hawaiian monk seals occasionally haul out here to rest; if you see one, stay 50 feet back. Camping is available with a state permit, and even on holiday weekends this beach feels deserted compared to anything in town.
